varieties.name: 1. Florida
varieties.traits: Compact rounded shrub, 1-1.5m H × 1m W, glossy dark green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Prolific bloomer, continuous flowering

varieties.name: 2. Magnifica
varieties.traits: Large shrub 1.5-2m H × 1.5m W, dense foliage, upright growth
varieties.yield_notes: Substantial flowers, peak flowering late spring-early summer
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for Brisbane subtropical climate, excellent for tall hedges and privacy screens, handles humidity well, ideal for frost-free coastal areas
varieties.availability: Platypus Plants, Australian Plants Online, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: 3. Radicans (Cape Jasmine Radicans)
varieties.traits: Dwarf ground cover, 30-45cm H × 80cm-1m W, small dark green glossy leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Abundant small flowers, extended bloom period

varieties.name: 4. Professor Pucci
varieties.origin: Named for Italian botanist Professor Pucci, modern cultivar
varieties.traits: Medium shrub 1.2-1.5m H × 1m W, glossy dark green elliptical leaves, bushy habit
varieties.yield_notes: Profuse flowering, generous bloom production

varieties.name: 5. Aimee Yoshiba (Aimee Yoshioka / First Love)
varieties.origin: Developed by Tadaoki Yoshioka in Hawaii 1950s, brought to Florida 1954, named for his daughter
varieties.traits: Large upright shrub 1.5-2.5m H × 1.5-2m W, darkest green foliage of all gardenias
varieties.yield_notes: Blooms twice yearly, generous flower production

varieties.name: 6. Golden Magic
varieties.traits: Tall shrub 1.5-2m H × 1m W, deep green leaves, upright growth
varieties.yield_notes: Pretty effect across hedging with multicolored blooms

varieties.name: 7. Four Seasons
varieties.traits: Compact shrub 60-90cm H × 60-90cm W, glossy green foliage, bushy
varieties.yield_notes: Flowers almost year-round (hence the name), continuous blooming
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for small Brisbane gardens, ideal for containers, pot culture, balconies. Compact size suited for limited outdoor spaces, thrives in subtropical warmth
varieties.availability: Australian Plants Online, Flower Power, widespread availability

varieties.name: 8. Ocean Pearl (Veitchii under new name)
varieties.origin: Old variety (Veitchii 1800s) renamed, discovered in China by Robert Fortune 1844
varieties.traits: Compact 50cm-1m H × 50cm-1m W, dense low-growing habit, glossy leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Flowers almost all year round (Nov-May peak), masses of blooms

varieties.name: 9. Lacey Lady (Lace Lady)
varieties.traits: Upright slender shrub 1.2-1.5m H × 0.8-1m W, glossy rich green foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Beautiful full white blooms throughout warm seasons
varieties.growing_notes: Classic low hedge for Brisbane gardens, very fragrant, perfect for frost-free subtropical spots, elegant romantic gift plant, upright form ideal for narrow spaces
varieties.availability: Australian Plants Online, The Plant Company NZ (ships to Australia)

varieties.name: 10. August Beauty
varieties.traits: Medium shrub 1.2-1.8m H × 1-1.2m W, compact rounded growth, glossy leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Long blooming period late spring through summer, heavy flower production

varieties.name: 12. Buttons
varieties.origin: Australian breeding program cross-pollination, mother plant 'Veitchii'
varieties.traits: Small rounded shrub 90cm H × 90cm W, forever glossy polished-looking leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Covers shrub with abundant small flowers, one of longest bloom seasons
varieties.growing_notes: Australian-bred for local conditions, perfect Brisbane borders, containers, patios, walkways. Compact size ideal for smaller subtropical gardens
varieties.availability: My Perfect Plants, Australian nurseries, widespread Queensland availability

varieties.name: 13. White Star (Australian Native Gardenia) 🌏
varieties.origin: Northern Queensland rainforests, indigenous Australian species, rare in wild and cultivation
varieties.traits: Low cascading groundcover 30-60cm H × 1m W, matt (not glossy) distinctively ribbed leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate flowering for native species
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS AUSTRALIAN SPECIES - rare true gardenia native to Queensland rainforests. Excellent for Brisbane native gardens, supports local biodiversity, adapted to subtropical humidity. Distinguishable by non-glossy ribbed foliage
varieties.availability: Australian Plants Online, specialist native plant nurseries Queensland

varieties.name: 14. Brown Gardenia / Native Gardenia (Atractocarpus fitzalanii) 🌏
varieties.origin: Queensland Australia, north of Mackay along Coral Sea coast rainforests
varieties.traits: Subtropical small tree/large shrub 3-8m H, broad rounded glossy leaves, distant coffee bush relative
varieties.yield_notes: Large bushtucker fruits (yellow mangosteen/brown gardenia)
varieties.growing_notes: INDIGENOUS QUEENSLAND SPECIES - native to Brisbane region rainforests. Listed by Brisbane City Council as recommended native plant. Attractive subtropical evergreen for humid frost-free gardens, supports Australian wildlife
varieties.availability: Brisbane City Council native plant program, Australian native nurseries

varieties.name: 15. Fortuniana
varieties.origin: China, sent by Robert Fortune to Royal Horticultural Society London 1844, historic cultivar
varieties.traits: Large shrub 1.5-2m H × 1.5m W, thick lustrous lance-shaped dark green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Most free-blooming cultivar, mid-spring through mid-summer

varieties.name: 16. Mystery
varieties.traits: Large upright shrub 1.8-2.5m H × 1.8-2.5m W, substantial growth habit
varieties.yield_notes: Impressive large blooms, spring flowering

varieties.name: 17. Belmont
varieties.traits: Large shrub 1.5-2m H × 1.2-1.5m W, exceptionally large glossy dark green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy blooming late spring-early summer, sporadic fall flowering

varieties.name: 18. Miami Supreme
varieties.traits: Large shrub or small tree form 1.8-2.5m H × 1.5-2m W, shiny lance-shaped dark green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Can bloom into fall, long season of fragrance and beauty
varieties.growing_notes: Good for Brisbane subtropical warmth (Florida-developed), can be trained as shrub or tree. Recommended by Florida extension = suitable for Queensland
varieties.availability: Specialty subtropical nurseries

varieties.name: 19. Kleim's Hardy
varieties.traits: Dwarf form 90cm-1m H × 90cm-1m W, distinctive star shape, bright yellow stamens
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable flowering
varieties.growing_notes: While bred for cold hardiness (Zone 7), performs well in Brisbane subtropical warmth too. Compact size perfect for smaller gardens, containers
varieties.availability: Perfect Plants, international availability

varieties.name: 20. Celestial Star
varieties.traits: Compact shrub 60-90cm H × 60-90cm W, thick lustrous dark green leaves, bushy habit
varieties.yield_notes: Profuse spring bloom, generous fall rebloom, heat tolerant
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect Brisbane patio/balcony plant, compact habit ideal for containers, deer resistant, pollinator friendly. Reblooming characteristic excellent for subtropical gardens
varieties.availability: J. Berry Nursery (international), specialty nurseries

varieties.name: 1. Gandhraj / Ananta (Gardenia jasminoides) 🏆
varieties.origin: Traditional variety in India for centuries, 'Gandhraj' = 'King of Fragrance' in Hindi
varieties.traits: Tropical evergreen shrub 1-1.5m H × 1m W, glossy dark green leaves, celebrated ornamental
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous blooming in tropical conditions

varieties.name: 2. Dikamali / Nadihingu (Gardenia gummifera) 🌏
varieties.origin: INDIGENOUS TO KERALA - Deccan Plateau and Peninsula of India, found in Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu. Traditional Ayurvedic medicinal plant
varieties.traits: Small deciduous tree/large shrub 3-8m H, greyish-brown bark exudes yellow resin (gummifera = gum-bearing), dark green shining leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Oblong berry fruits, medicinal yellow resin harvest
varieties.growing_notes: KERALA INDIGENOUS SPECIES - documented by Kerala Forest Research Institute Peechi. Found in degraded slopes, rocky strata in deciduous forests, dry laterite forests at Kerala Western Ghats foothills. Traditional medicine for wounds, skin diseases, fever, toothache, headache. Edible fruits. Deeply connected to Ayurvedic practices. Highly suitable for Thiruvalla region indigenous landscaping. Resin (Dikamali) has antimicrobial, antioxidant properties
varieties.availability: Kerala Forest nurseries, Ayurvedic plant suppliers, Kerala native plant specialists, found wild in Pathanamthitta district forests

varieties.name: 3. Florida (Gardenia jasminoides)
varieties.origin: Asia (China/Japan), cultivated in Indian gardens for generations
varieties.traits: Compact shrub 1-1.5m H × 1m W, glossy green foliage, well-adapted to tropical conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous flowering in tropical monsoon climate
varieties.growing_notes: Widely grown in Kerala gardens, thrives in Thiruvalla's warm humid conditions. Manageable size perfect for smaller spaces. Heat and monsoon rain tolerant. Popular for mixed plantings, containers
varieties.availability: The Plant Company, widespread Kerala nursery availability, common in Thiruvalla gardens

varieties.name: 4. Veitchii (Gardenia jasminoides)
varieties.origin: Historic 1844 introduction from China, adapted to tropical cultivation
varieties.traits: Compact plant 1-1.5m H × 1m W, small flowers, dense growth
varieties.yield_notes: Flowers 2-3 times per year in tropical conditions (more than temperate climates)
varieties.growing_notes: Recommended by Florida extension (similar tropical climate to Kerala). Performs excellently in Thiruvalla monsoon heat and humidity. Compact size ideal for Kerala garden spaces
varieties.availability: Specialty Indian nurseries, international plant suppliers

varieties.name: 5. Radicans (Gardenia augusta)
varieties.origin: Dwarf cultivar, well-established in tropical gardens
varieties.traits: Dwarf crawling growth habit 15-30cm H × 60-90cm W, small dark green glossy foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Abundant small blooms throughout warm season
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent Kerala ground cover, can be planted directly on ground due to spreading habit. Monsoon-adapted, tolerates heavy rain and humidity. Perfect for Thiruvalla rockeries, borders, tropical landscaping
varieties.availability: Spring Colours Australia (ships), Indian garden centers, Kerala nurseries

varieties.name: 6. Magnifica
varieties.traits: Tall shrub 2m+ H × 1.5m W, glossy leaves, substantial presence
varieties.yield_notes: Main flowering before monsoon season (pre-June)

varieties.name: 7. Aimee / Mary Ann (Gardenia jasminoides 'Aimee')
varieties.traits: Large shrub 2-3.5m H × 1.5-2m W, darkest green foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Later blooming (post-monsoon ideal), generous flower production
varieties.growing_notes: Florida recommends for good performance (tropical parallel to Kerala). Large showy blooms perfect for Kerala festivities. Later blooming suits monsoon cycle - flowers after heavy rains
varieties.availability: Top Tropicals (international tropical plant specialist), Kerala specialty nurseries

varieties.name: 8. August Beauty
varieties.traits: Medium shrub 1.2-1.8m H × 1-1.2m W, compact rounded growth
varieties.yield_notes: Long blooming period through summer, heavy flowering

varieties.name: 9. Belmont
varieties.traits: Large shrub 1.5-2m H × 1.2-1.5m W, exceptionally large dark leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Heavy late spring-early summer bloom, sporadic fall flowering

varieties.name: 10. Miami Supreme
varieties.traits: Large shrub/small tree 1.8-2.5m H × 1.5-2m W, shiny dark green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Blooms into fall season, extended flowering
varieties.growing_notes: Florida recommends for top performance (Florida climate = Kerala climate parallel). Can be trained as shrub or small tree form. Handles tropical heat and humidity. Spectacular for Kerala special occasions
varieties.availability: Tropical specialty nurseries, international plant importers

varieties.name: 11. Mystery
varieties.traits: Large upright shrub 1.8-2.5m H × 1.8-2.5m W, substantial growth
varieties.yield_notes: Spring flowering (pre-monsoon)

varieties.name: 12. Fortuniana
varieties.origin: Historic 1844 China discovery by Robert Fortune, heritage variety
varieties.traits: Large shrub 1.5-2m H × 1.5m W, thick lustrous lance-shaped dark green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Most free-blooming cultivar, mid-spring through mid-summer

varieties.name: 13. Buttons
varieties.traits: Small rounded shrub 90cm H × 90cm W, glossy polished leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Covers shrub abundantly, one of longest bloom seasons
varieties.growing_notes: Compact size perfect for small Kerala garden spaces. Ideal for containers on balconies, patios. Monsoon-tolerant with good drainage. Suitable for tropical heat
varieties.availability: International suppliers, specialty Kerala nurseries

varieties.name: 14. Four Seasons
varieties.traits: Compact shrub 60-90cm H × 60-90cm W, glossy green foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Flowers almost year-round (true to name in Kerala perpetual warmth)
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for Kerala small gardens, balconies, containers. Year-round blooming ideal for tropical climate where there's no winter dormancy. Compact well-suited to limited Thiruvalla urban spaces
varieties.availability: The Plant Company NZ, Kerala nurseries, Indian garden centers

varieties.name: 15. Golden Magic
varieties.traits: Tall shrub 1.5-2m H × 1m W, deep green foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Pretty effect with various colored flowers simultaneously
varieties.growing_notes: Visual interest for Kerala gardens, unique color progression. Heat and humidity tolerant. Good for hedging in tropical monsoon climate
varieties.availability: Specialty tropical nurseries

varieties.name: 16. Celestial Star
varieties.traits: Compact shrub 60-90cm H × 60-90cm W, dark green glossy leaves, bushy
varieties.yield_notes: Profuse spring bloom + fall rebloom (pre-monsoon + post-monsoon flowering)
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect Kerala balcony/patio plant, compact habit ideal for containers. Reblooming suits Kerala climate cycles. Heat tolerant, pollinator friendly
varieties.availability: International specialty nurseries, high-end Kerala garden centers

varieties.name: 17. Professor Pucci
varieties.origin: Named for Italian botanist, modern cultivar
varieties.traits: Medium shrub 1.2-1.5m H × 1m W, glossy dark green leaves, bushy
varieties.yield_notes: Profuse flowering spring-autumn, generous production

varieties.name: 18. Kleim's Hardy
varieties.origin: Cold-hardy cultivar (also performs in tropical heat)
varieties.traits: Dwarf form 90cm-1m H × 90cm-1m W, distinctive star-shaped flowers, bright yellow stamens
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable flowering
varieties.growing_notes: While bred for cold tolerance, also performs well in Kerala tropical warmth. Compact size perfect for smaller gardens, containers. Unique star shape distinctive in Kerala gardens
varieties.availability: International specialty nurseries

varieties.name: 19. Tahiti / Taitensis (Gardenia taitensis)
varieties.origin: Pacific Islands (Tahiti, Polynesia), traditional tropical species, 'Tiare' in Hawaii
varieties.traits: Compact evergreen shrub 1-1.5m H × 1m W, glossy dark green leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Continuous flowering in tropical warmth, spring-summer peak
varieties.growing_notes: TROPICAL ISLAND SPECIES perfectly suited to Kerala climate. Beloved in Hawaii = ideal for Thiruvalla tropical conditions. Native to tropical Pacific regions with similar climate to Kerala monsoon belt. Thrives in warm humid environments. Excellent for Kerala fragrant gardens, patios, borders
varieties.availability: The Plant Company, tropical specialty nurseries, Kerala high-end garden centers

varieties.name: 20. Thunbergia (Gardenia thunbergia)
varieties.traits: Large shrub/small tree 3-4.5m H × 2-3m W, striking evergreen, admired for glossy foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Showy cream-colored flowers, hard round fruits spread by animals
varieties.growing_notes: TROPICAL SPECIES suitable for Kerala warmth. Easy to grow, some gardeners use for blooming fragrant hedge. Can grow as small tree in Kerala landscapes. Dense multiple branches. Thrives in tropical conditions. Suitable for larger Thiruvalla properties
varieties.availability: The Plant Company, tropical plant specialists, Kerala nurseries
